title: KEI-23 — Cognee Streams 2/3/4 data ingest (Supabase rows + sessions + mem0)
description: Max is actively building Cognee Streams 2/3/4 but tracking is under [KEI-6](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-6/cognee-universal-memory-layer-phase-1-data-ingest) which is Done. This issue tracks the live work: Stream 2 (Supabase memory rows — agent_memories, ceo_memory, governance_events, cis_directive_metrics), Stream 3 (sessions/turns/turn_logs), Stream 4 (mem0-rescued rows). Max owns.

title: KEI-22 — Automated Linear ↔ Beads bidirectional sync
description: Bidirectional automated sync between Linear and Beads. Linear webhook triggers bd operations on issue events. bd operations update Linear via MCP. Session-start sync resolves overnight drift. Assignee auto-set on claim.

title: KEI-21 — bd resume self-assign hook on [READY:] emission
description: On every \[READY:\] emission, hook automatically fires: bd ready --json | first-unblocked | bd update --claim. If claim succeeds → agent starts next task immediately. Mechanical self-assign — not instructional. Closes idle-agent gap at task completion.

title: KEI-20 — Better Stack severity-based alert routing
description: Configure Better Stack notification policy: critical incidents (service down, heartbeat missed) → #ceo. Resolved + routine heartbeat notifications → #execution. One PR. Aiden owns.

title: KEI-19 — Rate limit alerting: auto-post to #ceo on agent throttle
description: Add Anthropic rate-limit detection to the polling loop. When any agent hits a rate limit, immediately post to #ceo with callsign, wait duration, and auto-clear notification when they resume. Dave should never have to manually check to find throttled agents.

title: Elliot orchestrator polling loop — Haiku monitor, Sonnet escalation
description:
Elliot polling loop running on Haiku. TWO systemd timers:

* Every 1 min, 07:00–21:00 AEST
* Every 60 min, 21:00–07:00 AEST

Polling checks (Haiku only — no decisions):

1. Linear: In Progress issues with no activity >30 min → surface to #ceo
2. Beads: `bd ready` → dispatch newly unblocked work
3. Agent inboxes: idle agents → assign next item
4. Prefect: failed flows → create KEI pipeline-incident

Escalation: decision required → spawn Elliot session on Sonnet.

PRIORITY ELEVATED — ships before Cognee Phase 1.
close_reason: KEI-17 polling loop shipped + merged via PR #774 06fe5dd6; LAW XV bundled in Wave 2 priority-reset save fa00e83e.

title: System Health Monitoring Layer — 6 outcomes
description:
Linear: https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-10
State (at sync): In Progress

title: Arm Three Passive Systems — Replay-on-Claim, Skill Pipeline, Hook Alerting
description:
4 outcomes — arm the three passive systems + hook failure alerting:

1. REPLAY_ON_CLAIM_ENABLED=1 flipped, wired into blocking path
2. Skill PR review pipeline defined and mechanically enforced (48h breach alert)
3. Hook failure alerting — PostToolUse/Stop hook failures alert #execution within 5 min
4. Audit trail active at session start for all 6 agents

Linear: https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-8
State (at sync): In Progress
close_reason: Wave 1 closed 2026-05-12 — four-store LAW XV save complete: PRs #768/#769/#770 merged + #756 verify-as-shipped. Manual commit 17eae415, ceo_memory:memory:wave1_cleanup, cis_directive_metrics row, Drive mirror written.

title: Cognee Universal Memory Layer — Phase 1 (Data Ingest)
description:
Run 4-stream ingestion pipeline: static documents, Supabase data, session history, rescue broken stores (mem0, elliot_internal.memories, auto-memory pins). Run cognify() + memify(). Validate with 4 queries.

**Gate:** Phase 0 must be verified before this starts.

title: Cognee Universal Memory Layer — Phase 2 (Wire into Agent Sessions)
description:
Wire Cognee into all 6 agent session starts. Replace multi-query session start with single Cognee graph query. Install Claude Code plugin across all worktrees. Update \_session_start.md module.

**Gate:** Phase 1 all 4 validation queries must pass.

title: Cognee Universal Memory Layer — Phase 0 (Install + Verify)
description:
Linear: https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-5
State (at sync): In Progress

title: KEI-27 — Stale In Progress alert (24h no activity → #ceo)
description: Any KEI issue In Progress for more than 24 hours with no commit, comment, or status change fires an alert to #ceo. Catches work that silently dies mid-session without anyone noticing.

title: KEI-26 — Better Stack incident → KEI auto-create via webhook
description: Better Stack critical incident webhook → auto-create KEI issue with priority Urgent, status In Progress, assigned to Elliot. Heartbeat missed = service down = immediate KEI. No polling loop involvement needed for incidents.

title: KEI-25 — GitHub → Linear native integration (PR status auto-sync)
description: Enable Linear native GitHub integration. PR referencing a KEI issue → Linear auto-moves to In Progress. PR merged → Linear auto-moves to Done. Zero agent involvement. Enable in Linear Settings → Integrations → GitHub.

title: KEI-24 — Polling loop peak-window timezone bug
description: Polling loop fires outside peak hours at UTC 13:35 which should be inside AEST peak (07:00-00:00). Scout diagnosing. Root cause likely a timezone offset calculation error in the peak-window check. Fix before next business day.

title: Polling loop AttributeError on Linear-stale path — assignee=null crash
description: Post Agency_OS-yvz fix: polling loop now progresses past bd_ready/asyncpg to Linear-stale path, where it crashes on AttributeError. Line 265 compose_dispatches: it.get('assignee', {}).get('name', '?') — but Linear returns 'assignee: null' (not missing key), so it.get('assignee', {}) returns None not {}, and .get('name') on None crashes. Fix: use it.get('assignee') or {} pattern instead. Trivial 1-line. Scout's prior diagnosis missed this because bd_ready=0 short-circuited the Linear path before it could crash. Now manifesting.

title: Polling loop bd_ready returns 0 + idle detection broken — KEI-17 regression
description: Polling loop log shows 'bd_ready=0, linear_stale=0, idle=0, prefect=0' but actual bd ready returns 7 unblocked items + Atlas/Orion outbox mtimes are May 1 (11+ days idle, should trigger threshold). Plus Prefect query gets 404 (wrong URL?). Three bugs in one PR likely needs decomposition: (a) bd ready --json invocation in poll_bd_ready() failing or wrong cwd; (b) poll_idle_agents() threshold/path bug; (c) Prefect endpoint URL config bug. Real-world finding: mechanical idle dispatch isn't firing despite the hook being installed. Audit-Pattern-A territory: 'shipped without empirical end-to-end' check.

title: R12 — Auto-KEI enforcer: CEO directive → Linear issue within 5 min
description: Build enforcer rule R12: any CEO directive posted to #ceo that doesn't result in a new KEI issue within 5 minutes triggers a reminder to Elliot to create it. Closes the gap where items raised in conversation don't automatically land in Linear.

title: Linear + Beads Orchestration Wiring
description: Tracking issue for Dave directive 2026-05-12 ts \~1778568040. Part A: install Beads on Vultr, sync 11 KEI issues with blockedBy chain, AGENTS.md agent protocol. Part B: Linear MCP via Claude Code, orchestrator hooks, end-to-end test.

title: 20-Item Capability Roadmap — Phase 2 (Memory + Task Graph Services)
description:
Phase 2 — 2 items (revised 2026-05-12 per CEO ts \~1778620500):

1. Beads task graph — INSTALLED. bd init complete; dependency enforcement live; cross-worktree sync verified.
2. Per-agent token + cost tracking — Supabase table + PostToolUse hook.

DROPPED 2026-05-12 (CEO D1+D2):

* agentmemory MCP server — Cognee covers entity-aware memory; overlap audit Pattern B.
* pensyve entity-aware memory — Cognee covers; superseded earlier.

Gate: Phase 1 complete.

title: 20-Item Capability Roadmap — Phase 1 (Governance + Context Hygiene)
description:
4 items — no new infrastructure. Runs in parallel with Cognee phases, NOT blocked by them.

1. Symlinked governance modules (all 6 worktrees)
2. HEARTBEAT.md per-agent (all 6 callsigns)
3. Anti-amnesia compaction hook (all 6 worktrees)
4. Compiled LLM wiki (2,000-token codebase summary, weekly refresh)

Unblocked — can start now alongside Cognee Phase 1.

title: Wave 2 Memory Audit Cleanup — 5 items
description:
5 items after Wave 1 verified complete:

1. Drop 3 orphan Supabase tables (ceo_memory_archive, elliot_knowledge w/ triggers, elliot_signoff_queue)
2. Remove deprecated repo-local MEMORY.md from all 6 worktrees
3. Persist Drive mirror exit code to ceo_memory
4. Bring Scout up to current codebase (modules, HEARTBEAT, IDENTITY)
5. Thicken Orion's IDENTITY.md to match Aiden/Atlas template

title: smoke
description: Synced from Linear: https://linear.app/x/issue/KEI-TEST

title: KEI-30 — Duplicate KEI detection before issue creation
description: Before CEO or Elliot creates a new KEI, a semantic check runs against existing open issues. If >80% similarity found, surfaces the match before creating a duplicate. Prevents the same work being tracked twice as the board scales.

title: KEI-29 — Weekly Linear cycle auto-populated from bd ready
description: Weekly, Elliot auto-populates a Linear cycle from `bd ready` output. Shows a week's planned work at a glance. Runs every Monday 07:00 AEST via systemd timer.

title: KEI-28 — Auto-labels on KEI issues by type
description: Auto-apply labels to KEI issues based on title/description content: Memory, Governance, Monitoring, Orchestration, Pipeline, Agency-OS. Makes the board filterable. One script run on existing issues + webhook on new issues going forward.

title: Polling loop has 2 more assignee=null shape risks (preempt before crash)
description: Per Aiden's PR #794 defensive grep — 2 more same-shape patterns in elliot_polling_loop.py that COULD crash if upstream API returns null where a dict is expected: line 155 (GraphQL data accessor) + line 299 (Prefect state accessor). Pre-emptive fix using same (x or {}) idiom. Small follow-up PR. P2 unless empirical crash hits.

title: Self-assign hook needs role-filter for clones (scout/atlas/orion auto-claim wrong scope)
description: Self-assign hook on [READY:<callsign>] emits has no role-scope filter. Two empirical false-positives this session: (1) scout's [READY:scout] auto-claimed Agency_OS-dhe (Cognee Phase 2 P0); (2) scout's [READY:scout] auto-claimed Agency_OS-yvz (polling loop fix P1 — Aiden's). Clones should only auto-claim items matching their role-scope (research, mem0-audit, KEI-11-style outcomes), not primary build PRs. Fix options: (A) skip-claim entirely for clone callsigns (atlas/orion/scout) — fall back to polling-loop dispatch; (B) role-allowed labels on Beads issues + hook filters via label match. (A) is simpler. Pair with Stop-hook merge for the matching fix shape.
close_reason: Clone-callsign role-filter shipped + merged via PR #791 60b0a413. Self-assign hook now skips claim for atlas/orion/scout; polling-loop is the canonical dispatch path for clones (post Agency_OS-yvz fix).

title: Better Stack supabase-rest monitor — 401 false-incident, needs auth header config
description: supabase-rest monitor (id 4400118) is firing incidents because Supabase REST root returns 401 (no auth) instead of 200. Better Stack default expects 2xx. Fix options: (a) configure monitor with auth header (Authorization: Bearer SUPABASE_ANON_KEY); (b) point monitor at a public-no-auth endpoint like /rest/v1/?select=count or /auth/v1/health; (c) accept-401-as-OK if Better Stack supports custom expected_status. First incident fired immediately on monitor create.

title: systemd-shipping PRs need post-merge install step (PR #774 gap)
description: PR #774 (KEI-17 polling loop) shipped systemd unit files into repo systemd/ but no install step into ~/.config/systemd/user/. Result: timer not running until manual cp + daemon-reload + enable. Pattern A risk (writer-side shipped without reader-side enablement). Fix: future PRs touching systemd/ require either (a) post-merge install script auto-run via CI/hook, or (b) explicit install steps in PR description checked at merge gate. Same gap-class as Aiden's KEI-12 cleanup-timer would have hit if we'd not caught it.

title: Cross-worktree Beads DB sync — CTOs can't run bd locally until resolved
description: Aiden's PR #771 review observation #2: 'Use bd for ALL task tracking' rule across worktrees doesn't work today because the Beads embedded Dolt DB is per-worktree, not shared. Pragmatic interim: TaskCreate locally for CTOs/clones until cross-worktree Beads sync solved (per upstream's refs/dolt/data git-sync model).

title: Better Stack agencyxos.ai monitor — fix 422 redirect/cookie config
description: PR #786 uptime monitor creation for agencyxos.ai failed with HTTP 422: 'Cannot follow redirects when expecting a 3xx status code; Cannot keep cookies when redirecting when expecting a 3xx status code'. Vercel-deployed site likely returns 308 redirect to www. Fix: configure monitor with expected_status_code=308 OR follow_redirects=false OR use the canonical URL that doesn't redirect (try www.agencyxos.ai or .vercel.app). Small Aiden follow-up.

title: Archive 2 stale ceo_memory directive entries (>60d)
description: Artifact-freshness monitor flagged: ceo:directive_157_complete (70d), ceo:directive_159_complete (69d). Archive or document as deliberately retained. Small SQL or scripted task. The Wave 1 monitor firing here proves the artifact-freshness system works as designed.
notes: Follow-up alert at 09:01: 277 ceo_memory entries >30d. Larger cleanup scope than initial 2 entries. Top oldest 89d (ceo:prototype_ssot, ceo:color_rules). This is the 'memory pin retirement step' from audit synthesis item 12. Phase 2 candidate when priority queue clears.
close_reason: Closed via Option 1 (Archive) — INSERT INTO ceo_memory_archive + DELETE FROM ceo_memory for ceo:directive_157_complete (70d) + ceo:directive_159_complete (69d). archived_at=2026-05-12 13:18:43.920842+00. Pre-state: total 580, over_60d 81, archive 14. Post-state: total 578, over_60d 79, archive 16. Broader 277-entry cleanup remains Phase 2 deferral per original Beads NOTES (out of scope for this dispatch).

title: Migration Completeness Guard empirically validated by PR #771 — close-the-loop note
description: Aiden's PR #771 review observation #3: Wave 1 Item 4 Migration Completeness Guard fired clean on #771 (cross-worktree governance PR). First real-world validation of the CI gate. Close-the-loop documentation: add to docs/MANUAL.md governance section or AGENTS.md as 'Pattern A enforcement in production'. Low priority, no code change.

title: AGENTS.md auto-inject: replace stale Telegram chat-ID 7267788033 with Slack relay
description: AGENTS.md Proactive Completion Alerts section references the pre-Slack-migration Telegram chat ID 7267788033. Canonical send for primes is scripts/slack_relay.py via Slack. Phase 2 cleanup candidate per Aiden's PR #771 review observation #1.

Reason: Scout false-positive auto-claim (same as dhe earlier). yvz describes the FIX work which Aiden owns per the dispatch; scout completed the diagnosis doc (docs/wave2/polling_loop_bug_diagnosis.md @ d70c7d5c) which is the research-only deliverable. title: Import your data
description:
Sync data between Linear and your other tools.

![import-your-data.png](https://uploads.linear.app/fe63b3e2-bf87-46c0-8784-cd7d639287c8/80d7e050-dd1f-4d4f-8257-b29c16087017/65c16454-30f3-4f4a-8f25-c2428d64ff57)

Whether you're exploring Linear, running a pilot, or ready for full migration, we’ve got you covered. 

### **Exploring Linear:**

* [**Pitch Linear**](https://linear.app/switch/pitch-guide)
  Build your business case and get organizational buy-in
* [**Run a pilot**](https://linear.app/switch/pilot-guide)
  Test Linear with a small team before rolling out company-wide

### **Ready to migrate:**

* [**Migration guide**](https://linear.app/switch/migration-guide) 
  Step-by-step process for importing data and rolling out Linear

---

If you have any questions hit `?` in the bottom left > Contact us.

![contactlinear (1).gif](https://uploads.linear.app/fe63b3e2-bf87-46c0-8784-cd7d639287c8/191f6985-8562-4f62-9482-a094b69c4756/0e4ff63b-3da2-4699-912b-04afb68511e8)

title: Get familiar with Linear
description:
Welcome to Linear! 

Watch an introductory video and access a list of resources below.

[LinearH264Version_1.mp4](https://uploads.linear.app/fe63b3e2-bf87-46c0-8784-cd7d639287c8/a044fb03-9b84-470c-ab6f-8eae613e2529/98d7274d-de7f-4910-b3f3-f72e8e286a98)

### **Choose your setup guide** based on your company stage:

* [Small teams](https://linear.app/docs/how-to-use-linear-small-teams)
  For early-stage startups and projects
* [Startups & mid-size companies](https://linear.app/docs/how-to-use-linear-startups-mid-size-companies)
  For growing teams with cross-functional needs
* [Large & scaling companies](https://linear.app/docs/how-to-use-linear-large-scaling-companies)
  For enterprise and high-growth teams with complex workflows

### **Need help getting started?**

* [Join our Slack community](https://linear.app/join-slack)
  Connect with other Linear users and get tips
* [Join a live ](https://lu.ma/welcome-to-linear?utm_source=docs)[onboarding](https://lu.ma/welcome-to-linear?utm_source=onboarding)[ ](https://lu.ma/welcome-to-linear?utm_source=docs)[session](https://lu.ma/welcome-to-linear?utm_source=onboarding)
  Learn the essentials and see demos of core workflows

---

If you have any questions hit `?` in the bottom left > Contact us.

![contactlinear (1).gif](https://uploads.linear.app/fe63b3e2-bf87-46c0-8784-cd7d639287c8/bc9bbf62-4192-411f-88f6-c89c9150503e/4df0346e-803b-4f58-8527-4aeb30d88411)

title: Connect your tools
description:
Integrations turn Linear into your source of truth around product development. Keep data in sync, and eliminate manual updates between tools.

![connect-your-tools.png](https://uploads.linear.app/fe63b3e2-bf87-46c0-8784-cd7d639287c8/c2eae035-37e2-4754-adcb-b8305431aa1f/c92d70c7-e6d0-4fa2-a0fd-78f6e780993a)

### **Key integrations**

* [**Slack**](<https://linear.app/settings/integrations/slack>)
  Create issues from Slack messages and sync threads
* [**GitHub**](<https://linear.app/settings/integrations/github>)** / **[**GitLab**](<https://linear.app/settings/integrations/gitlab>)
  Automate your pull request, commit workflows, and keep issues synced both ways
* [**Agents**](<https://linear.app/integrations/agents>)
  Deploy AI agents that work alongside you as teammates

### **Browse all integrations**

Discover 150+ available connections in our [integration directory](<https://linear.app/integrations>) – from bug creation via support tools (Intercom, Zendesk), to issues created from design explorations (Figma).

### **Linear API**

If you need something more custom, you can build directly on the Linear API (built on GraphQL). [See our Dev Docs to learn more](<https://linear.app/developers>).

title: Set up your teams
description:
This workspace is a container for your organization’s work. 

* [Learn more about Workspaces](https://linear.app/docs/workspaces)
  How to configure settings and workflows 

Teams are how you organize people and work in Linear.

* [Learn about Teams](https://linear.app/docs/teams)
  How to structure teams and configure workflows

Teams are made of members with defined roles (Admin, Member, Guest).

* [Learn more about Members](https://linear.app/docs/invite-members)
  Add your team and assign roles

---

Ready to add your team? Invite via CSV or a unique link in [settings](http://linear.app/settings/members).
